title: Lord's Universal Form | Bhagavad Gita Chapter 11 Verses 14- 21 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So, the title for today's discussion, the topic is The Universal Form.
So, today we will be discussing based on the teachings, the purpose of His Divine Grace
Srila Prabhupada.
Text 14.
Then, bewildered and astonished, His hair standing on end, Arjuna began to pray with
folded hands, offering obeisances to the Supreme Lord.
This is after the Lord has shown the Universal Form to Arjuna.
So, this is what Arjuna is going through.
He is bewildered, he is astonished, his hair standing on end.
And when a person is in extreme ecstasy, you know, sometimes the hair...
hair stand on end, began to pray with folded hands, offering obeisances to the Supreme Lord.
Now, till now, Arjuna had a relationship of the Lord as his friend, as his chariot driver,
as his charioteer.
And when he saw that he is not an ordinary person, he is not an ordinary friend, he showed
the Universal Form which has...
all the universes, all the devatas, all...
as we move forward, we will see what all he saw.
Then, when he saw that, it is that very same Lord who has become the chariot driver, has
become like a menial servant.
He has taken on the menial job of driving my chariot.
You know, he had...
he was astonished, he was bewildered.
He couldn't fathom what he is seeing.
So, he folded his hand.
He was in ecstasy, and paid obeisances.
Now, his natural loving friendship was overwhelmed with wonder.
What he was seeing, he was unable to believe.
He was wonder-struck.
Arjuna watched.
So, Arjuna said, my dear Lord Krishna, I see assembled together in your body, all the demigods.
All the different Devi and Devatas, and various other living entities.
I see Brahma sitting on the lotus flower, as well as Lord Shiva, and many great sages
and divine serpents.
So Universal Form of the Lord is all-encompassing.
Everything what we see, higher planets, the lower planets, different living entities,
different kinds of Devatas.
All were...
were...
so to say, there in the Universal Form, and he saw that form.
And his vision, his ability to see that Universal Form was the divine grace.
The Lord gave him that vision.
The Lord gave him the ability to fathom or to see that Universal Form.
The causeless mercy of Lord Vishnu is unparalleled, and such mercy is perceived by the devotee
only by the grace of the Lord.
So even to fathom the Lord, to understand the Lord, we started our discussion today
with that, so to say, point of view.
To understand the Lord, we have to get the mercy of the Lord.
So far, whatever we have discussed, anyone has any question, anything you'd like to ask
about the initial part, or what we started on?
Any questions?
Anything you'd like to ask about the initial part, or what we started on?
Any questions?
Anything you'd like to ask about the initial part, or what we started on?
Any questions?
Anything you'd like to ask about the initial part, or what we started on?
Anyone has any question, anything you'd like to ask?
Yeah, the others were also seeing the form, but specifically, what Arjuna saw, maybe what
they saw is different from what Arjuna saw.
He was, as we move forward in this discussion, it says, Arjuna saw because of the divine
vision given to him by the Lord.
So others may not have been given that.
They saw something, but what they saw, was it the same form, Universal Form, which Arjuna
saw?
It's not clearly mentioned.
It's not clearly mentioned.
It's not clearly mentioned.
Who was saying it?
Who was saying it?
Who was saying it?
In the Gita?
In the Gita?
No, it is not true.
90 minutes was the duration in which the Gita was spoken.
It's not true.
Others were also hearing.
Others were also seeing Krishna.
I'll come to that part, as to why Krishna spoke the message of Gita in the battle field.
And that too, at a very crucial juncture, where the battle is about to start.
And that too, at a very crucial juncture, where the battle is about to start.
where the battle is about to begin. There is a message for all of us. Why it was spoken
there? It could have spoken somewhere else. O Lord of the universe, I see in your universal
body many, many forms, bellies, mouth, eyes, expanded without limit. There is no end, there
is no beginning and there is no middle to all this. In other words, it's unlimited,
infinite. There is no start and end. There is no beginning and an end. Now Krishna is
the supreme personality of Godhead and he is unlimited. He is the source of matta sarvam
pravartate, aham sarvasse prabhavo. Krishna says in the Gita, everything is infinite.
Everything is emanating from me. Aham sarvasse prabhavo, matta sarvam pravartate, iti matva,
one who has that understanding. Matva means the understanding. Matta means belief. Bhajante
maam, worships me, glorifies me. Buddha bhava samanvitaha. Buddha means such an intelligent
person, such a wise person. Bhava samanvitaha.
With devotion, with bhava, with feelings, not sentimentally, not ritualistically, but
having the right understanding, that supreme personality of Godhead, who is the source
of everything, everything is emanating from him, the all-powerful, limitless, infinite
Lord. When we have the tattvata understanding, the natural consequence of that understanding
will be, we will surrender to him.
Now some frequently asked question when it comes to God, is God a person? A lot of people
believe in God. You ask, do you believe in God? Yes. What is your understanding of God?
So a lot of people say there is some power, some energy, some divine force, some supreme
controller.
But it is vague. What is that divine? What is divine? So you have the dual Revelation,
The person Dis pope, and me, before that, the self is dominated, off the streets, by the
divine, divine authority and the will of god, that is order. Because I am alive, i am self,
and whether i am able to ever breathe him, that will not be possible in jahman, just that word will
What is that supreme controller?
And second frequently asked question is, does God have a name?
Is it a vague energy, divine force, supreme power?
Is he a person? Does he have a well-defined name?
Just like you and me, we are a person.
We have an identity, we have a name.
So is it some specific thing or it's a vague concept?
It's a belief.
Now, since God is unlimited, we talked about Krishna being unlimited, infinite,
his names also must be unlimited.
Everything about the Lord, his form is unlimited, his names are unlimited,
his energies are unlimited, his potencies are unlimited,
his creation is unlimited.
Since God is unlimited,
his names also must be unlimited.
Therefore, we cannot settle on one name.
God has many names.
Can anyone help me with the names of God?
So Prabhupada explains,
for instance, Krishna is sometime called Yashodanandan,
the son of Mother Yashoda.
Any other names of God?
Or Devakinandan, the son of
Devakinandan.
Or Vasudevanandan, the son of Vasudeva.
Or Nandanandan, the son of Nandamahara.
Sometime he is called Parthasarathy.
So there are unlimited names of God.
Infinite names of God.
And all these names, because of many dealings of God with his devotees,
according to those dealings, he is called by different names.
His name, Krishna.
Krishna, Krishna means to attract.
His principal name, Krishna, the name, the word Krishna,
Krishna, Krishna, Krishna means to attract.
And Lord, because he is infinite, he has infinite opulences,
he is Shad Aishwarya Purnam, he is all attractive.
Now, when we see many pictures of Krishna, we see that he attracts even the cows,
calves, birds, bees, trees, plants, even the water in Vrindavan.
He is not attractive only to human beings, only to some, this thing, even to other living beings.
He is all attractive.
And that was demonstrated when Krishna personally descends in Vrindavan.
He is attractive to the Cahod boys, to the Gopis, to Nandamaharas, to Pandavas and to all human society.
Now Parasramuni, who is the father of Vedavyasa, he gave a very, very precise definition of God.
Now, it is not just a vague understanding of God, very precise definition.
Just like in science we study, if it is an electron, there is a definition for that.
Or if there is any particular object which we are studying, we try to define it, to make it very specific.
It is not left very vague.
So, what is the definition of God?
He defines God as,
a person who is Shad Aishwarya Purnam.
Shad means six, Aishwarya means opulences, Purnam means complete.
Aishwarya means a person who has unlimited opulence.
Who is full in six opulences, who is full in strength, fame, wealth, knowledge, beauty and renunciation.
So, if you find a person who has these opulences, to an unlimited extent, he is God.
Now, let's go one by one, each opulence.
We see in this world, there are many rich people, am I right?
There are many people like rich people,
like we have Bill Gates,
now we have, who will become a millionaire, himself a millionaire,
Michael Jackson,
and celebrities whose earnings are in millions and billions.
And because they are rich, they are attractive.
May not be attractive because of their facial beauty.
Because of their opulence.
If Bill Gates were to come to India or to Mumbai,
people will throng to go and hear him out.
Or if somebody happens to meet him, they will feel very fortunate, very blessed.
If somebody gets an autograph, I have Bill Gates' autograph.
So, he is attractive because of his opulence.
Now, Bhagawan,
just we see a person who has finite opulence,
which is also fleeting, which is also temporary,
we have good, we see it in real life that person becomes attractive,
people get attracted to him.
Now, visualize a scenario, a person who has unlimited opulence,
he can proudly say, or rather he can boldly say,
all what we see is my opulence.
That's why, I think two classes back I said,
only two people can say, everything what you see is my opulence, is mine.
What are the two people?
One, a madman can say that, and God can say that.
Everything is coming from me.
Everything belongs to me.
I am the proprietor.
Matta, Sarvam, everything is emanating from me.
So, Bhagawan, the Supreme Personality of Godhead is the proprietor of all riches.
Everything what we see, actually he is the proprietor.
There are many rich men in the world who attract the attention of public,
but no one can claim that he possesses all the wealth in the world.
No one can claim.
But God claims that.
Krishna, now he gave some demonstrations of his richness or opulence.
He had 16,108 wives and each wife lived in a separate palace.
Palaces were built with first class marble.
Pillars of palaces were made of kural.
So, there is a description in the Bhagavatam about the palaces in Dwaraka.
Krishna expanded himself into 16,108 forms
and was personally present with each wife in each palace
at the same time.
This is the opulence of the Lord.
It is not that he married 16,108 wives.
By the way, a question when many people ask,
why did he marry so many women?
He was not happy with one.
What's the answer?
Yes, Sunil.
So, this was a very big...
You know, when I was relatively new to Krishna consciousness,
I used to discuss with my friends in the office.
And this was a very big...
difficult thing for me to handle.
Why did Krishna dance with the gopis?
I used to ask, why did he do it?
Okay, I accept he is God,
but why did he do all these things?
Now, we are in problem
to answer all these things.
So, we have very nice understanding.
So, it was not that 16,108 wives,
and then one night with one wife,
and another day with another wife,
and another day with another...
16,108 palaces.
And it was simultaneous.
It was opulent.
He was God.
He is all-powerful.
He was simultaneously present
in all those palaces,
with each wife,
in every palace at the same time.
God is omnipotent.
He can maintain not only 16,000 wives,
but 16 million,
and still encounter no difficulty.
Unlike present time.
One is also difficult.

We see lot of strong people,

Muhammad Ali,

well-built personalities,
very strong.

has unlimited strength.
Now, this strength what we see,
in the so-called strong people,
is fleeting.
It's temporary.
Yes or no?
With passage of time,
even the so-called strong people,
you know, become very weak,
frail,
old,
diseased,
and finally die.
Bhagawan, the Supreme Personality of Godhead,
has unlimited strength.
His strength was present
from the moment of his birth.
It's not that he became God.
When Pootana came to kill Krishna,
she was a very big demoness.
She was only three months old.
He effortlessly killed the demon Pootana.
When Krishna descended 5000 years back,
enacted various pastimes,
this was like small trailer,
to give demonstration.
These are all not fictitious stories.
This is not fiction.
Even when you go to Vrindavan,
these places of pastimes are there,
where Govardhan Hill is there.
He gave that demo just to demonstrate
that how he is that same Supreme Person.
When Lord Krishna was seven years old,
he lifted Govardhan Hill on the little finger
on his left hand for seven days effortlessly.
God is God from beginning.
Krishna does not become God
by some meditation
or by some mystic power.

ok, we as devotees of Krishna may say,

many millions in the world

The fame of Bhagavad Gita is Krishna's fame.

something which the Lord spoke,

which was again spoken 5000 years back,
which has stood the test of time.
Although things have changed,
times have changed,
the world has evolved,
our understanding of world has evolved,
still,
if you just read the Gita,
you will be enamoured
by the wisdom,
the knowledge which it contains.
And it has stood the test of time.
Beauty.
We see a lot of beautiful people,
but again, beauty is fleeting.
And because of
their beauty,
they are attractive.

The planet is an insignificant planet

beautiful scenery,
landscapes,
creation,
yet within one planet,
one country,
America,
has so many attractive features.
Just imagine,
how many attractive features must be possessed
by God,
who is the creator of entire cosmic manifestation.
How beautiful He must be,
He who has created all beauty,
who is the source of all beauty.
When Krishna, in the 10th chapter,
talks about His opulences,
He says,
this is all spark of His splendour.
All gigantic, beautiful creations,
what you see,
is just a small spark of His splendour.

We have a lot of people

very eminent scientists,

They become attractive.

has all knowledge,
to an unlimited degree.
A scientist or philosopher may be attractive

than that given by Krishna in the Gita?
Bhagavad Gita is universally renowned
jewel of India's spiritual wisdom.
Having talked about Gita,

when I read Bhagavad Gita

how God created this universe,
everything else seems so superfluous.
When we talk about Gita,
there are,
I think at this right time today,
we can talk a little bit about
the common misconceptions
which people have about Gita.
Before I address couple of them,
can anyone help me with
one or two possible misconceptions
about Gita which people have?
Possible misconceptions, yes.
You are right.
So some people believe it,
we should not keep Gita in home.
Just like in the Gita,

the war is happening.

Okay.

Everything is fiction,
it is mythology.
Indian mythology.
Any other misconceptions
which we generally hear of
when it comes to Gita?
Okay, I'll come to couple of them
which I heard many times.
First thing that many people say,
it's post-retirement study material.
Abhi toh time hai, zindagi jeene ka, enjoy karne ka, career banane ka, har cheez ka time hota hai.
Jab retire ho jaoge, aur kuch karne ko nahi hai, nothing better is left in your life, then read Gita peacefully.
Actually, Gita is all about how to lead a meaningful and successful life, a dynamic life, in consciousness of God.
It is like a manual.
When a person is on the deathbed with five breaths to, you know, left, that is not the time to be reading Gita.
Another thing.
Another thing is, if we have not cultivated the right, so to say, inclination, so taste for hearing Gita or this subject matter,
we see many old people who have all the time in the world, but they have no inclination.
Many of us want to read Gita, but we may find, you know, Swamiji, I don't get time, what to say, I have to leave early in the morning, I come back late,
I have so many other obligations, duties, I want to read.
I have a destination.
I have a desire to read, but I don't get time.
Somebody may say like that, but there are a lot of people who have all the time in the world, but they have no taste for reading Gita.
The taste has to be cultivated.
In fact, Bhagavatam says,
From the very childhood,
means from the childhood, we should cultivate this knowledge of God.
So,
it is not post-retirement study, otherwise Krishna wouldn't have spoken to Arjuna, who is a Kshatriya, in the battlefield at a very crucial juncture when the battle is about to begin.
He wouldn't have wasted his time and Arjuna's time and the time of all the people who had assembled in the battle of Kurukshetra.
Second thing which people have a misconception is that Gita means once you start reading Gita, you will become a sanyasi.
You will have to give up everything, you will become completely renounced, you will leave your family, leave your house, and you will go to the forest, and they are scared.
One time, this happened many, many years back.
I was, you know, we used to distribute Gitas during the books marathon time, so I was also distributing Gita, so I was having a Gita in my hand and
there was a place where we had put a stall, then I saw a young man coming and then I said, yes, I should be somehow giving this Gita to him.
He should get this Gita, that was my desire.
You know, sometimes you desire something, you have a wish, he should somehow get it.
So, as he was approaching, I also, you know, started moving few steps towards him and indicated this Gita.
So, the moment he saw that, he, you know, like a shock, as if, you know,
some Cobra is there in my hand, he stepped back, he says, no, no, no, I said, this is Gita, it will transform your life, he says, that's what I don't want.
Because transformation, they have a different understanding of what is transformation, transformation will become like us.
Swamiji, he says, that's what I don't want, and he stepped back, as if, you know, got a shock of his life and didn't take the Gita.

So, a lot of people have this conception that if you start reading Gita, you will give up everything, which is far, far from the truth, because that's what Arjuna wanted to do in the first chapter of the Gita.
What did he say? I don't want to fight, I will beg and live, I don't want kingdom.
In fact, he was doing...
He was trying to be a sanyasi.
He wanted to renounce the kingdom, I don't want this kingdom, I don't want this fight.
I don't mind living by begging.
The whole Gita was spoken for what?
So that he should not give up.
He should do his duty, he is a Kshatriya, he should fight.
So, this is a misconception.
The Gita teaches that we should absolve ourselves and act irresponsibly and give up our duties and responsibilities.
No.
We have to execute those duties and responsibilities with right consciousness.
Krishna is everything.
Mam anusmarya yudhya ca.
You be conscious of me, think of me and fight.
You are a Kshatriya, do your duty.
Arjuna was a family man.
Krishna chose to speak to Arjuna.
99.99% of this population in this world is the rest are family men.
Very rare, some very unique species are there like us.
Yes or no?
So, Gita objective is not to make people like us.

No doubt, that's a separate discussion why some people, some very rare soul will get that inspiration, that desire, that aspiration, the Lord will choose them maybe for a higher purpose to deliver this message to others.
Had Prabhupada not taken it up as a missionary zeal, maybe we won't be sitting here today discussing Gita.
So, Gita does not teach that you give up everything, give up your family, give up your job, give up your this thing and go to the forest.
It doesn't teach that.
It teaches how we can make our life successful, how we can balance our material and spiritual duties intelligently
and lead a Krishna conscious life and thereby make our life successful.
Meaningful, purposeful.
If we just simply lead our lives, even animals are leading their lives.
Animals also are taking care of young ones. Animals are also eating, sleeping, mating, defending.
We may do in a much more sophisticated, royal, so elaborate ways but that doesn't qualify us to be human beings.
We have got developed intelligence for a higher purpose.
To understand God, to understand Krishna.
The higher questions of life, who we are, where we have come from, what is the purpose of life, where do we go after death,
what is this material existence, what is the spiritual world, who is God, what is our relationship with Him,
how we can revive our lost relationship with Him.
That is what Gita is teaching us.
And we can do that while we are engaged in our worldly duties.
Krishna does not.
In fact, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu says,
Stane sthite.
Stane sthite means wherever you are, whatever your circumstances are.
Stane sthite. Sthite means be there.
Shruti gataam, but give a hearing to this message of Gita and Bhagavata.
Give a submissive hearing.
With that what will happen?
That taste will revive.
That consciousness of Lord which has got lost will get revived.
Another misconception which Gita has, it's not for, it's predominantly for, you know, it's a scripture, it's a religious book.
It is meant for people like us Swamijais, Sadhus, they should be dabbling.
It is their textbook.
It is their study material.
It is not meant for householders.
As I mentioned, there is a reason why Krishna spoke to Arjuna, who is a householder, who is a Kshatriya.
So it is meant for all of us.
And it teaches about how we can lead our lives with that nice delicate balance, balancing our spiritual and material duties.
The last and the sixth opulence, renounce.

Similarly, Krishna's potencies are working under the direction of His assistants, the devatas.
The Vedic literature asserts that God has many names, but Krishna is the principal name.
Why Krishna is the principal name?
Because of unlimited opulence, unlimited fame, unlimited beauty, unlimited wealth, unlimited renunciation, unlimited knowledge.
At the same time, to an unlimited degree, He becomes all attractive.
Just like by one opulence, somebody has lot of money, becomes attractive.
Somebody who is very beautiful becomes very attractive.
He has unlimited opulence and hence the name Krishna.
The Lord has many names.
He can be called by many names, but the principal name is Krishna because He is all attractive.

Ask.

The universal form which Krishna showed to Arjuna is not a spiritual form.
It's a material form and it is also not eternal.
There are two things.

It is not eternal.
It is not spiritual.
So, the universal form also is a temporary form which was shown to Arjuna just to give him a glimpse of his opulence.
It is not a spiritual form.
It is not an eternal form.
Your form, adorned with various crowns, clubs and discs, is difficult to see because of its glaring effulgence,
which is fiery and immeasurably like the sun.
You are the supreme primal objective.
You are the best in all universes.
You are inexhaustible and you are the oldest.
You are the maintainer of religion, the eternal personality of Godhead.
You are the origin without beginning, middle or end.
You have numberless arms and the sun and moon are among your great unlimited eyes.
By your own radiance, you are heating the entire universe.
